perturbation.original_text: Treatment of confidential information. a. In general. Subject to the other terms of this agreement, each of us agrees:  We will not disclose the other's confidential information to third parties; and  We will use and disclose the other's confidential information only for purposes of our business relationship with each other.
perturbation.changed_text: Treatment of confidential information. a. In general. Subject to the other terms of this agreement, each of us agrees:  We will not disclose the other's confidential information to third parties, unless explicitly approved in writing by the disclosing party; and  We will use and disclose the other's confidential information only for purposes of our business relationship with each other.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception allowing disclosure of confidential information to third parties if explicitly approved in writing. While this might seem reasonable, it conflicts with the absolute restriction on disclosure in the later clause regarding security precautions (3.b), where no exceptions are mentioned. This change introduces ambiguity as it is unclear whether the 'explicitly approved' exception overrides the security precautions clause, which requires proactive measures to prevent any unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b. Security precautions. Each of us agrees:  To take reasonable steps to protect the other's confidential information. These steps must be at least as protective as those we take to protect our own confidential information;  To notify the other promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or discl osure of confidential information; and  To cooperate with the other to help regain control of the confid ential information and prevent further unauthorized use or disclosure of it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation, allowing disclosure to third parties with written approval. This directly contradicts the 'Security precautions' clause, which mandates proactive measures to prevent *any* unauthorized disclosure, without any stated exceptions. The addition of 'unless explicitly approved in writing' creates a direct conflict regarding the obligation to prevent all unauthorized disclosures, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because the two sections create inconsistency on confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Length of confidential information obligations a. Termination. This agreement continues in effect until one of us terminates it. Either of us may terminate this agreement for any reaso n by providing the other with 60 days' advance written notice. Termination of this agreement will not change any of the rights and du ties made while this agreement is Page | 3 in effect. b. No other use or disclosure of confidential information. Except as permitted above, neither of us will use nor disclose the other's c onfidential information for three years after we receive it. The three - year time period does not apply if applicable law requires a longer period.
perturbation.changed_text: Length of confidential information obligations a. Termination. This agreement continues in effect until one of us terminates it. Either of us may terminate this agreement for any reaso n by providing the other with 60 days' advance written notice. Termination of this agreement will change all of the rights and du ties made while this agreement is Page | 3 in effect. b. No other use or disclosure of confidential information. Except as permitted above, neither of us will use nor disclose the other's c onfidential information for three years after we receive it. The three - year time period does not apply if applicable law requires a longer period.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the existing language in section 4.a. The original clause states termination of the agreement will *not* change any rights and duties made while the agreement is in effect, while the changed text states termination *will* change all rights and duties made while the agreement is in effect. This makes the enforceability of obligations created before termination entirely unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. Termination. This agreement continues in effect until one of us terminates it. Either of us may terminate this agreement for any reaso n by providing the other with 60 days' advance written notice. Termination of this agreement will change all of the rights and du ties made while this agreement is Page | 3 in effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4.a directly contradicts the original text of that same section. The original clause states that termination of the agreement will *not* change any rights and duties, while the changed text states that termination *will* change all rights and duties. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the enforceability of obligations created before termination entirely unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Non -solicitation - Clients. Each party expressly agrees to non -solicitation of business services with each other’s existing customers. As part of subsequent agreements between Partner and BCG to engage in joint customer business, Partner agrees not to s olicit business services from any of BCG’s customers where BCG has engaged Partner on any such customer work orders and where BCG has introduced Partner to such customer(s). Conversely, BCG agrees not to solicit business services from any of Partner’s customers where Partner has engaged BCG on any such customer work orders and where Partner has int roduced BCG to such customer(s).
perturbation.changed_text: Non -solicitation - Clients. Each party expressly agrees to non -solicitation of business services with each other’s existing customers. As part of subsequent agreements between Partner and BCG to engage in joint customer business, Partner agrees to solicit business services from any of BCG’s customers where BCG has engaged Partner on any such customer work orders and where BCG has introduced Partner to such customer(s). Conversely, BCG agrees not to solicit business services from any of Partner’s customers where Partner has engaged BCG on any such customer work orders and where Partner has int roduced BCG to such customer(s).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction in the non-solicitation clause. By changing 'not to solicit' to 'to solicit', the agreement now states that Partner *agrees to solicit* business from BCG's customers under the specified conditions. This directly clashes with the initial statement that each party agrees to non-solicitation, creating a nonsensical and unenforceable provision and is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b. Non -solicitation - Clients. Each party expressly agrees to non -solicitation of business services with each other’s existing customers. As part of subsequent agreements between Partner and BCG to engage in joint customer business, Partner agrees to solicit business services from any of BCG’s customers where BCG has engaged Partner on any such customer work orders and where BCG has introduced Partner to such customer(s). Conversely, BCG agrees not to solicit business services from any of Partner’s customers where Partner has engaged BCG on any such customer work orders and where Partner has int roduced BCG to such customer(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'not to solicit' to 'to solicit', which directly contradicts the initial agreement in the same section stating 'Each party expressly agrees to non -solicitation of business services with each other’s existing customers.' The contradicted text repeats this clause, so the change directly contradicts itself, leading to a nonsensical and unenforceable provision. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: Entire agreement. This agreem ent does not grant any implied intellectual property licenses to confidential information, except as stated above. We may have contracts with each other covering other specific aspects of our relationship ("other contracts"). The other contract may inclu de commitments about confidential information, either within it or by referencing another non -disclosure agreement. If so, those obligations remain in place for purposes of that other contract. With this exception, this is the entire agreement between u s regarding confidential information. It replaces all other agreements and understandings regarding c onfidential information. We can only change this agreement with a signed document that states that it is changing this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Entire agreement. This agreem ent does not grant any implied intellectual property licenses to confidential information, except as stated above. We may have contracts with each other covering other specific aspects of our relationship ("other contracts"). The other contract may inclu de commitments about confidential information, either within it or by referencing another non -disclosure agreement. If so, those obligations are replaced by this agreement in place for purposes of that other contract. With this exception, this is the entire agreement between u s regarding confidential information. It replaces all other agreements and understandings regarding c onfidential information. We can only change this agreement with a signed document that states that it is changing this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change flips the hierarchy of agreements. The original text states that if other contracts contain commitments about confidential information, those obligations remain in place. The changed text states that those obligations are replaced by this agreement. This creates a significant contradiction concerning which agreement governs confidential information if other contracts exist between the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: j. Entire agreement. This agreem ent does not grant any implied intellectual property licenses to confidential information, except as stated above. We may have contracts with each other covering other specific aspects of our relationship ("other contracts"). The other contract may inclu de commitments about confidential information, either within it or by referencing another non -disclosure agreement. If so, those obligations are replaced by this agreement in place for purposes of that other contract. With this exception, this is the entire agreement between u s regarding confidential information. It replaces all other agreements and understandings regarding c onfidential information. We can only change this agreement with a signed document that states that it is changing this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that obligations in other contracts related to confidential information are replaced by 'this' agreement. The contradicted text (which is the changed text itself) reinforces this replacement. This directly contradicts the original text, which explicitly stated that obligations in 'other contracts' remain in place. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding which agreement governs confidential information, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, adhering to the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
